([Estonian](/wiki/Estonian_language "Estonian language"): *Tallinna Linnahall*, 'Tallinn City Hall'; originally the **V. I. Lenin Palace of Culture and Sports**) is a multipurpose venue in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). It is located in the harbor, just beyond the walls of the Old Town, and was completed in 1980. The venue also features a heliport and a small seaport.

([ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **EECL**), is a heliport at the Linnahall in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). It was used by [Copterline](/wiki/Copterline "Copterline") for its flights to [Helsinki Hernesaari Heliport](/wiki/Helsinki_Hernesaari_Heliport "Helsinki Hernesaari Heliport") in [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki"), [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"). It is the only publicly used heliport in Estonia.

**Linnahall** ([Estonian](/wiki/Estonian_language "Estonian language"): *Tallinna Linnahall*, 'Tallinn City Hall'; originally the **V. I. Lenin Palace of Culture and Sports**) is a multipurpose venue in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). It is located in the harbor, just beyond the walls of the Old Town, and was completed in 1980. The venue also features a heliport and a small seaport.

## Venue

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Linnahall&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Venue")]

The [1980 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1980_Summer_Olympics "1980 Summer Olympics") were hosted in [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"). As inland Moscow had no suitable venue at which to stage the [sailing event](/wiki/Sailing_at_the_1980_Summer_Olympics "Sailing at the 1980 Summer Olympics"), this task fell to [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), the capital of the then [Estonian SSR](/wiki/Estonian_SSR "Estonian SSR").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Apart from the main venue for the sailing event, [Pirita Yachting Centre](/wiki/Tallinn_Olympic_Yachting_Centre "Tallinn Olympic Yachting Centre"), a number of other sports and entertainment facilities were erected,[[2]](#cite_note-2) among them the V. I. Lenin Palace of Culture and Sport.[[3]](#cite_note-3) designed by architects [Raine Karp](/wiki/Raine_Karp "Raine Karp") and Riina Altmäe.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The skating rink closed in 2009, followed by the concert hall in 2010. The city sought investors from 2009 to 2015, and in 2015 the city council decided to renovate Linnahall, although the project did not come to fruition.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

From June to July 2019, the location served as a stand-in for a [Kyiv](/wiki/Kyiv "Kyiv") opera house during the filming of the feature film *[Tenet](/wiki/Tenet "Tenet")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

It is not to be confused with the primary administrative building of the historical municipal government (Raad) of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), often referred to as the [Town Hall](/wiki/Tallinn_Town_Hall "Tallinn Town Hall").

**Linnahall Heliport** ([ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **EECL**), is a heliport at the Linnahall in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). It was used by [Copterline](/wiki/Copterline "Copterline") for its flights to [Helsinki Hernesaari Heliport](/wiki/Helsinki_Hernesaari_Heliport "Helsinki Hernesaari Heliport") in [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki"), [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"). It is the only publicly used heliport in Estonia.
